prompt: A Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of Katsushika Hokusai's 'Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji' series (c. 1830–1833). The scene depicts a steep mountain path with a lone figure descending, stopping at regular intervals to examine the ground with a measuring stick. Each stopping point is marked by a small depression or marker, showing the path of exploration. The figure wears traditional Edo-period clothing. The composition uses dramatic diagonals, flat areas of unmodulated color in indigo, ochre, and muted green, with visible woodgrain texture throughout. Bold black outlines define all forms. Atmospheric perspective shows the valley below fading into mist. The path behind the figure curves and becomes visible at different altitudes, showing the entire descent trajectory. No text, no labels, no diagrams.
tradition_approach: Targeted Hokusai's compositional diagonals, flat unmodulated color fields, woodgrain texture, bold outlines, and atmospheric perspective — using the mountain descent as a natural gradient descent metaphor
